latest.title: Exploration Progress at WA Projects
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3960L
Cloudbreak Discovery PLC
08 July 2026
8 July 2026
Cloudbreak Discovery Plc
('Cloudbreak', or 'the Company')
Exploration Progress at WA Projects
Cloudbreak Discovery Plc (LSE: CDL),
the London Stock Exchange Main Market-listed mineral exploration company
("CDL"),
is pleased to provide an update on exploration activities across its portfolio of projects in Western Australia, including progress on permitting, heritage agreements and the commencement of planned exploration programmes.
Heritage Agreements
The Company has now executed Heritage Protection Agreements covering each of its three Western Australian projects, representing an important milestone in advancing exploration activities while ensuring continued engagement with Traditional Owners.
Darlot West Project
The Darlot West Project, set out at Figure 1 has been reviewed and analysed for the proposed drilling programme as a result of the multiple targets identified from the geological and structural interpretation, shown at Figure 2. The location of the drill holes have now been approved for an initial 3,000-5,000 metre aircore drilling programme at the Darlot West Project, see Figure 3. The programme has been designed to test key structural targets identified through recent geophysical interpretation and surface exploration.
The Programme of Work (
"
POW") submitted to the Western Australian Department of Mines, Petroleum and Exploration has been approved, and a Heritage Protection Agreement has been signed with the Watarra Aboriginal Corporation.
The remaining requirement prior to drilling is completion of a heritage survey to obtain final clearance for the proposed drill sites. The Company has formally requested the survey and is working closely with the Watarra Aboriginal Corporation to complete this process as soon as practicable. Subject to receipt of heritage clearance, drilling is expected to commence shortly thereafter.
Paterson Project
A Heritage Protection Agreement has been executed with the Jamukurnu-Yapalikurnu Aboriginal Corporation.
The Company has submitted a heritage survey request to facilitate a helicopter-supported Mobile Magnetotelluric (
"
Mobile MT") survey. Heritage clearance is expected in due course, following which the Company will update shareholders on the anticipated timing of the geophysical survey.
Crofton Project
A Heritage Protection Agreement has also been signed with the Nyamal Aboriginal Corporation, and an application for heritage clearance has been submitted to enable the next phase of surface geochemical exploration at the Crofton Project.
Following a prolonged period during which site access was restricted, field activities are expected to commence during the next quarter. The programme will comprise detailed geological mapping, rock-chip sampling of historical soil anomalies returning greater than 25 ppb gold, together with an extensive soil sampling campaign, shown at Figure 4. The objective of this work is to better define the extent of the mineralised system, generate additional exploration targets and prioritise areas for drill testing later in the year.
Appointment Consultant Geologist
The Company is pleased to announce the appointment of Dahrouge Geological Consulting Ltd. ("Dahrouge") as consulting geologist to support its exploration activities in Western Australia.
Founded in 1971, Dahrouge is an internationally recognised geoscience and project consultancy serving the global mineral exploration and mining industry. With more than five decades of experience, the firm provides multidisciplinary expertise across exploration, resource modelling, project management and technical advisory services, supporting projects from early-stage exploration through to development.
The appointment further strengthens CDL
'
s technical capability as it advances exploration across its portfolio.
Tom Evans, Cloudbreak's MD, commented; "
This marks an important milestone for Cloudbreak as we transition from planning to execution across our Western Australian exploration portfolio. With heritage agreements now in place for all three projects and key approvals progressing, we are well positioned to commence significant field activities in the coming months.
At Darlot West, recent geophysical interpretation has identified a number of compelling drill targets, and we look forward to commencing our maiden aircore drilling programme following completion of the final heritage survey. At the same time, work continues to progress at both the Paterson and Crofton projects, where planned geophysical and geochemical programmes are expected to further refine priority targets ahead of future drilling.
Our strategy is to systematically unlock value across a portfolio of highly prospective exploration assets. With multiple exploration programmes scheduled throughout the remainder of 2026, we believe CDL is entering one of the most active phases in its development. As these programmes advance, we expect to deliver a steady pipeline of operational milestones, exploration results and news flow, and we look forward to keeping shareholders updated as we execute our exploration strategy
."
Figure 1: Location of 60.6km2 Darlot West Gold Project, Western Australia - 10km from 2.8Moz Darlot Gold Mine, and 25km from Thunderbox Gold Mine operated by Northern Star Limited.
Figure 2: Geological and structural interpretation based on high resolution magnetics, with multiple targets identified.
Figure 3: Proposed Drill hole locations for Drill program at Darlot West
Figure 4: Crofton Project rock chip and soils results, with planned soil survey outline.
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
-END-

Cloudbreak Discovery PLC

About Cloudbreak Discovery PLC
LSE listed Cloudbreak Discovery PLC is a leading gold, precious and base metals resource explorer. Cloudbreak is focused on mineral exploration in Western Australia with the aim of bringing near-term cashflow and driving shareholder value. Through its wholly owned subsidiaries, the Company will develop its array of mineral assets, whilst continuing to generate new projects with a particular focus on commodities with high intrinsic value. Cloudbreak's generative model across the mineral sector enables a multi-asset approach to investing in the commodity cycle.
Competent Persons Statement
The Information in this report that relates to exploration results, mineral resources or ore reserves is based on information compiled by Mr Edward Mead, who is a Fellow of the Australasian Institute of Mining and Metallurgy. Mr Mead is a consultant to Cloudbreak Discovery Plc and employed by Doraleda Pty Ltd. Mr Mead has sufficient experience which is relevant to the style of mineralisation and type of deposits under consideration and to the activity that he is undertaking to qualify as a Competent Person as defined in the 2012 edition of the `Australian Code for Reporting Exploration Results, Mineral Resources and Ore Reserves' (the JORC Code). Mr Mead consents to the inclusion of this information in the form and context in which it appears in this report.

latest.title: EIS Geophysics Application Granted at Paterson, WA
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9579E
Cloudbreak Discovery PLC
20 May 2026
20 May 2026
Cloudbreak Discovery Plc
('Cloudbreak', or 'the Company')
EIS Geophysics Application Granted at Paterson, WA
Cloudbreak Discovery PLC (
LSE
:
CDL
), a London Stock Exchange Main Market listed company, is pleased to advise that an application for a co-funded geophysics program has been granted covering the Company's
Paterson Project
located ~40km south of the Telfer Gold-Copper Mine and ~60kms SW of the Havieron Gold-Copper Mine in the Paterson region of Western Australia ("
Paterson
") (
Figure 1
).
·
Geophysics program award under the West Australian Government's Exploration Incentive Scheme (EIS) covering the Paterson Project
·
Co-funded geophysics grant of up to $269,270 ($438,540 project overall cost, 50% funded by government plus a one-off payment of up to $50,000) to undertake widespread, semi-regional, MobileMT Surveying
·
MobileMT surveying in the Paterson region has already proven to be effective in mapping resistivity contrasts, structure and geological complexity / direct targets under considerable cover depths
·
Cloudbreak has built a large, strategic landholding within the Paterson region (~888km
²
) which is deemed prospective for copper-gold-molybdenum mineralisation.
The Paterson Project represents a strategic 888km² land package in Western Australia's premier exploration frontier, located just 40km from the 15-million-ounce Telfer Gold-Copper Mine. With compelling historical drill results (Cu, Au and Mo) and multiple untested geophysical targets, this project offers substantial discovery potential in a region renowned for major mining operations and new discoveries.
Commenting on the EIS Co-Funding grant, MD Tom Evans said:
"We are delighted to secure EIS co-funding support from the Western Australian Government for this major MobileMT program across our Paterson Project. The survey represents a significant step forward in systematically unlocking the potential of our 888km² landholding in one of Australia's most prospective copper-gold districts.
The MobileMT program will provide critical regional-scale geological and structural data beneath cover, helping us refine existing targets and identify new priority areas for drilling. Importantly, the grant allows us to accelerate exploration while materially reducing the cost burden to shareholders.
With increasing industry attention on the Paterson region following several major discoveries, we believe Cloudbreak is strongly positioned to advance a large-scale exploration strategy in a highly sought-after mineral province.
Importantly, the Company remains fully funded for its planned exploration activities, including the upcoming Darlot drill campaign, which I look forward to updating the market on shortly."
Commenting on the EIS Co-Funding grant and geophysical technical aspects, Consultant Geophysicist Russell Mortimer said:
"Co-funded, systematic MobileMT surveying over the Paterson Project will greatly enhance/support geological and structural mapping to deep level across the full tenement package, with the ultimate aim to highlight and confirm prospect scale priority targets supported by all other available geophysical data/methods"
Figure 1
- Paterson Project - Regional Location/Tenement Package Plan
MobileMT Survey and Forward Program
The proposed MobileMT AEM survey encompasses approximately 2,771 line kms. Coverage is proposed to be acquired at 400m spaced survey lines (flown NE-SW) to provide effective exploration for large mineral systems (Figure 2). Line spacing is believed to be suitable for detection of sulphide bodies/alteration systems of significant areal size/footprint at reasonable exploration depth under cover. Planned survey lines are designed to be roughly perpendicular to the average geological strike, which is believed optimal. Survey height/sensor height is proposed to be ~60-80m above ground level. This modern passive AEM survey is appropriate for the exploration target/geological model, as it has the ability to rapidly map structure, conductors/resistors (relative resistivity contrasts) under significant cover/overburden over broad project areas. Such surveying has been very valuable for a number of exploration groups within the Paterson region in recent years to provide direct exploration vectors - St George, Wishbone and Rincon.
The survey will be flown by Expert Geophysics in the coming months with their proprietary MobileMT AFMAG system and interpretation will be completed by Russell Mortimer of Southern Geoscience Consultants (SGC).
MobileMT is an airborne natural-field electromagnetic technology that maps subsurface resistivity by measuring variations in naturally occurring EM signals over a broad frequency range. The heliborne system is designed for broadband acquisition (26 Hz to 21 kHz), providing sensitivity to both shallow and deep structures. The resulting products are geology-meaningful resistivity images that support 3D resistivity mapping, structural interpretation, and targeting.
Figure 2
- Paterson Project - Proposed MobileMT Survey Coverage 2026 over Regional Aeromagnetic Imagery
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
-END-

Through its wholly owned subsidiaries, the Company will develop its array of mineral assets, whilst continuing to generate new projects with a particular focus on commodities with high intrinsic value. The information presented herein that relates to Exploration Results from information compiled and reviewed by Russell Mortimer, a Competent Person who is a Member of The Australian Institute of Geoscientists and fairly represents this information. Mr Mortimer has sufficient experience relevant to the style of mineralisation and type of deposit under consideration, and to the activities undertaken, to qualify as a Competent Person as defined in the 2012 Edition of the Joint Ore Reserves Committee (JORC) Australasian Code for Reporting of Exploration Results, Mineral Resources and Ore Reserves. company_30s.what_they_do: Cloudbreak Limited is a junior Australian gold miner that acquires, drills and develops high‑grade gold projects in Western Australia.  Its main assets are the Paterson, Crofton and Darlot West sites, where it has already produced strong assay results and is working toward commercial production.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company operates in the world’s most prolific gold districts, where the price of gold remains a safe‑haven asset.  Its projects have some of the highest grades in the region (e.g., 65 g/t at Darlot West), giving it a clear competitive edge over smaller explorers.  If it can move from exploration to production, it could become a significant contributor to Australia’s gold output.
company_30s.current_state: Cloudbreak is in a development phase: it has recently secured EIS funding, completed a major acquisition (Paterson Project) and is drilling the Crofton and Darlot West sites.  Cash burn is high, but the company’s recent positive assay results are a good sign of progress.  The share is trading around 0.6 p, a steep discount to its previous valuation.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with corporate and financing moves – AGM, share admission, EIS geophysics approval and a fresh option exercise to acquire Crofton – all aimed at strengthening its capital base.
Exploration activity is ramping up: positive gold grades have been announced for Crofton and Darlot West, and the Paterson acquisition adds a proven deposit to the portfolio.
Strategically, Cloudbreak is consolidating its focus on Western Australia, divesting non‑core assets and positioning itself for a future production phase.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent optimism – traders are quietly watching for the first positive assay or funding announcement.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter centers on the high grades at Darlot West and the acquisition of Paterson.  Several posts praise the company’s potential to become a producer and call the current price a bargain.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A few voices warn that the company’s projects are still in early drilling stages, that funding could be delayed, and that the gold market could weaken.  They stress the long timeline to production.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat but is shifting slightly bullish as the company’s recent results lift expectations.  The overall mood remains cautious yet hopeful.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, so any meaningful news could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The first assay results from the Crofton drill hole – if they confirm the 30‑plus g/t grade, it would validate the project’s economics.
Official confirmation of the EIS grant and the funding it unlocks – this would give the company the cash to accelerate drilling.
A formal announcement of a production timeline or a new shareholder offering – both would signal confidence in the project’s future.
A volume spike around the 0.5‑0.6 p support level, indicating capitulation is complete and buyers are stepping in.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained 20‑day moving‑average crossover, a 3‑day volume surge, and any positive drill‑hole announcement.  If the share stays below 0.5 p for a month with flat volume, the bounce may stall.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle – the APEX score is high, indicating tight compression.  Confirmation of capitulation (volume collapse) and a clear catalyst (drill results or funding) are needed before the mean reversion can kick in.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The setup qualifies as a RED (ultra‑crash) signal, historically delivering around 160 % returns when a catalyst triggers a rebound.  If the price recovers to the midpoint of its previous range (≈1.3 p), the upside is roughly 2‑3× the current level – a highly asymmetric payoff.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are project delays, funding shortfalls, or regulatory setbacks that could push the share further down.  A worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to 0.3 p, a 50 % loss from the current price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk is limited to the drop from 0.6 p to 0.3 p (≈0.3 p).  Potential upside to 1.3 p is ≈0.7 p, giving a risk‑reward ratio of about 2.3:1.  The asymmetry is clear – the downside is capped while the upside is substantial.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at 0.6 p with a 20 % position size of your equity.  Place a stop just below 0.5 p to protect against a false break.  Target 1.3 p; trail the stop to 1.1 p once the price surpasses 1.0 p.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a 20‑day MA cross, a 3‑day volume surge, or a positive assay announcement.  Once any of these trigger, enter as outlined above.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risk has surfaced yet, so there is no reason to abandon the position at this stage.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Cloudbreak sits at a deep crash level, with a bullish drilling catalyst on the horizon and a clear 2‑3× upside versus a modest downside – a contrarian long that rewards patience.
